data.body:
# Jira-sync test-coverage hardening — retro, research, epic, and plan-review

## What this session produced

Created epic **f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0** — "Close the Jira-sync producer<->consumer & tested-vs-shipped test-coverage gap class" — with six child stories, then ran the plan-review remediation loop to a clean PASS.

## Origin: three sync bugs fixed earlier this session (provenance)

- **0ee6** (inbound comments): inbound differ read snapshot key `comments`; fetcher writes nested `comment`. Fixed (PR #31, merged).
- **3f04** (issue-links both directions): outbound batch applier dropped links (only the dead typed-leaf applier handled them); inbound snapshot never carried `issuelinks`. Fixed (PR #36, merged).
- Plus commit-gate hardening (PR #33) and a parent-hierarchy-limitation doc (PR #37) — all merged.

## Retro — why the bugs escaped (5 root causes)

1. Tests mock at the wrong seam: differs unit-tested against hand-built snapshot dicts in shapes the real fetcher never emits (flat `comments`; `issuelinks` the snapshot never carried). Producer (fetcher+enrichment) and consumers (differs) never jointly exercised — no producer<->consumer contract.
2. Tested the non-production path: outbound link/comment/label handling was tested on the typed leaf `_apply_outbound_update`, but production routes through the batch `update_one`. Capabilities re-added one bug at a time (parent 8b25, comments 87e4, links 3f04).
3. A live probe encoded the bug as expected: `e2e_field_validation_probe.sh:1543-1547` `pass_test "...inbound-comments-gap NOT-SYNCED"` — passed on the defect.
4. Live probes are manual, not in CI, and don't round-trip relationships; `bridge-probe` only does create/label/property/search/delete.
5. Silent-success telemetry: batch outcome reports mutation-level `error=None`, never `links_applied`/`comments_applied`; a sub-op no-op leaves the pass green.

## Deep research (incorporated into the epic)

Converged best practices for third-party-API / Jira integration testing without change-detector bloat:
- Producer<->consumer contract (Pact = "VCR in reverse"; consumer generates the contract so it can't drift).
- Record/replay kept honest via semantic matchers + secret-scrubbing (vcrpy), not whole-blob equality.
- Schema validation asserting structure/type not values (Schemathesis, Dredd/Gavel).
- "Verified fakes" (Google SWE-book): the fake carries contract tests run against BOTH real and fake.
- Real Jira clients diverge: jira-python uses a live sandbox; jira-cli serves on-disk fixtures captured from real Jira THROUGH the production serialization path (an in-process mock). The latter is the small-team-fit pattern.
- Avoid change-detector tests (Google SWE-book): no mock-call-count, no golden-blob equality; assert behavior/round-trip/invariants.

Recommendation baked into the epic: ONE producer<->consumer snapshot contract test, fed by hermetic-but-honest fixtures derived from real Jira payloads, exercised through the PRODUCTION path. Full live-sandbox e2e and cross-team Pact judged overkill for a single team.

## Epic structure (six stories, sized for one PR each)

- A (fe57-7712-1e3f-45f4): Hermetic-but-honest Jira fixtures + verified fake client (foundation).
- B (8937-eed3-c881-4ea1): Producer<->consumer snapshot contract — shared schema + round-trip test through production path (keystone; owns the documented anti-change-detector guardrail). depends_on A.
- C (59ef-ff3e-5aea-47b1): Verified fake — contract tests against BOTH real and fake client. depends_on A.
- D (33d0-1e2c-e059-4181): Collapse the dual outbound applier paths to one.
- E (2359-ac1a-7c76-4f56): Sub-op telemetry + silent-no-op canary. depends_on D.
- F (822a-2bad-fe48-43e0): Honest bidirectional round-trip probe; de-encode "expected-bug" guards. depends_on A.

Epic links: discovered_from 0ee6 and 3f04. MVP-first sequencing (A->B on the comment/issuelinks seam first), plus a Rollout & rollback section (fail-loud invariant lands warn-first behind a flag; applier collapse verifies parity before deleting the retired path).

## Plan-review remediation loop

- Round 1: PASS, 0 blocking, 10 advisory. Remediated all valid findings: added E->D ordering edge; split compound AC into 12 atomic lines + reworded vague "appropriate"; added Rollout & rollback (warn-first flag + parity-before-delete on D/E); added Child-stories decomposition list; added MVP sequencing; assigned the guardrail-doc/ADR deliverable to keystone B (the 3x roster finding). Declined the configurable-threshold advisory with justification (YAGNI; exposed counts in the structured outcome instead).
- Round 2: PASS, 0 blocking, 1 advisory (subjective word "clean" in prose) -> reworded "clean baseline" to "a baseline pass that emits zero such warnings".
- Round 3 (bonus confirmation): timed out at the tool level (exit 124, LLM latency / rate-limiting) — no verdict. Not retried; round 2 had already met "no significant changes advised" and the only outstanding item was the cosmetic word fix, which was applied.

Net: 10 -> 1 (trivial) advisories; plan PASSES; epic is in final state (12 atomic AC lines, all sections present, 6 children parented).

## Follow-ups / open items

- Round-3 review confirmation never returned a verdict (tooling timeout). If a signed clean attestation is wanted, re-run `rebar review-plan f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0` when LLM latency is lower.
- Epic + children are open and unclaimed, ready to execute MVP-first (A -> B on the comment/issuelinks seam).
